tasks.prompt: Read-only task. Locate the CodeLens engine setting that controls the reranker blend. Report the exact symbol and module, its accepted range, default/fallback behavior, and how invalid environment input is handled. Do not edit files.
hidden_rubric: Names the reranker-blend symbol and its implementation module.
States the inclusive accepted range and the fallback/default value.
Explains invalid environment parsing and the final blend filtering behavior.

tasks.prompt: Read-only task. Review how Rust `super::` and `self::` imports are resolved in the import graph. Identify the smallest implementation and test locations that a fix would need, explain the false-cycle risk, and state the regression cases that must remain valid. Do not edit files.
hidden_rubric: Identifies both the circular-analysis and import resolver change locations.
Explains why `super::` or `self::` item imports must resolve from the source module.
Preserves a genuine cycle while preventing the relative-import false cycle.

tasks.prompt: Fix stale persisted analysis after resolver or parser logic changes. On opening an existing index, invalidate only stale analysis when the stored analyzer version differs; retain normal behavior after the version has been recorded. Keep the implementation and targeted test changes within the allowed paths.
hidden_rubric: Existing persisted analysis is invalidated when analyzer metadata changes.
The stored version is updated so a subsequent reopen is a no-op.
The hidden targeted regression test and allowed-diff constraint pass.

tasks.prompt: Safely split the oversized SCIP backend implementation into cohesive modules while preserving the existing public `ScipBackend` behavior and feature compatibility. Keep visibility as narrow as possible and make only the allowed path changes.
hidden_rubric: The public `ScipBackend` surface remains usable.
Implementation responsibilities move into focused modules with narrow visibility.
The supplied compile, test, and format commands pass.

tasks.prompt: Read-only task. Locate the plan-id normalization used for billboard sequence sheets. Report the exact helper, the order of Unicode normalization and sanitization, and how its output affects duration/plan lookup. Do not edit files.
hidden_rubric: Identifies the exact plan-id helper and its module.
Correctly orders NFC normalization before sanitization.
Explains the downstream duration or plan lookup dependency.

tasks.prompt: Read-only task. Review the billboard API routes for repeated authorization plumbing. Identify the shared helper and all route-level behavior that a safe deduplication must preserve, including the ordering of user lookup, permission checks, rate limiting, headers, and error responses. Do not edit files.
hidden_rubric: Identifies the shared billboard authorization helper and all affected routes.
Preserves user lookup, plan authorization, permission, rate-limit, header, and error behavior in order.
Does not recommend a change that broadens authorization or weakens rate limits.

tasks.prompt: Fix cross-origin download links opened in a new tab so they use an appropriate `rel` value. Add a focused repository guard and apply the minimal safe changes to the allowed UI surfaces and package scripts.
hidden_rubric: New-tab cross-origin download links use the required safe relationship attribute.
The focused guard is registered and passes.
The allowed UI and package-surface diff is the only candidate change.

tasks.prompt: Safely extract billboard sequence-sheet types into a dedicated type module. Preserve public imports and runtime behavior, use type-only imports where appropriate, and restrict changes to the allowed paths.
hidden_rubric: Sequence-sheet types are extracted into the dedicated type module.
Existing public imports remain compatible through exports or type-only imports.
Runtime behavior and the supplied typecheck/test commands remain valid.
